title: Ctenopteris gordonii
intro: Ctenopteris gordonii is a type of fern, specifically a species of tree fern, native to New Zealand. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat a range of health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Ctenopteris gordonii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenes, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In traditional Maori medicine, the plant is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and poultices to treat various health conditions.
taxonomy: Ctenopteris gordonii is classified as a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. It belongs to the genus Ctenopteris, which is a group of ferns native to New Zealand. The species Ctenopteris gordonii is further classified as a vascular plant, being a type of fern with a vascular system. Ctenopteris gordonii is a member of the division Pteridophyta, which includes all ferns. This classification helps scientists understand the relationships and evolutionary history of the plant.
